notes.anchor: you did not render thanks to the Senate
notes.note: After the victory at Mutina the Senate had voted Lepidus honours (including a gilded equestrian statue); Cicero reproaches him for failing to send the customary formal letter of thanks (gratiarum actio) for them.

notes.anchor: the reconciliation of peace between citizens
notes.note: Lepidus was offering to mediate a settlement with Antony; Cicero warns that any "peace" restoring Antony to power would be servitude, not concord. Within weeks Lepidus instead joined his army to Antony's.

notes.anchor: a ruined man into possession of an utterly intolerable despotism
notes.note: The "ruined man" (perditus homo) is Antony, whom Cicero casts as a desperate would-be tyrant; restoring him to power would mean dominatio (autocratic mastery) over the state.
